dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, you might be the only person in {location} who doesn't have a stake in this feud. That's exactly why I need you.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Two families here have been at each other's throats for generations over a boundary line that nobody can even find anymore. The problem is, {target_npc} from one side and a friend of mine from the other have been meeting in secret, sharing tools and stories. If their families find out, both of them will be cast out. I need {quest_item} from a meeting place {location_hint} to prove that cooperation works, before the {enemy_type} pressing on our borders make the whole argument pointless.
dialogueChunks.outro: If those two get caught before we can show their families there's a better way, we lose the only bridge between them.

playerResponses.text: I'll find what you need. This feud has gone on long enough.
Playing peacemaker between angry families? Fine, but I expect compensation.
Family feuds aren't my problem. Sort it out yourselves.

dialogueChunks.intro: I need someone who doesn't care about local politics, {player_name}. Someone who judges people by what they do, not where they come from.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A traveler arrived in {location} two weeks ago and the people here won't give them the time of day. Wrong accent, wrong clothes, wrong everything according to the elders. But {target_npc} has been helping this stranger quietly, and now the council is threatening to strip {target_npc} of their standing. If I can gather {quest_item} from {location_hint} and show the council that outsiders bring value, maybe they'll listen to reason. The {enemy_type} don't care about borders, and neither should we.
dialogueChunks.outro: One good deed from a stranger could undo a lifetime of prejudice. But only if we act before the council meets.

playerResponses.text: Prejudice is a poison. I'll help you cure it.
Changing minds isn't easy. What's in it for me when this works?
Local politics are exactly what I try to avoid. Good luck.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, have you seen the wall between our settlement and the one across the ridge? It wasn't always there, and it doesn't need to stay.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Years ago, a dispute over {quest_item} led the elders to build a barrier cutting off the path between {location} and the neighboring settlement. Families were split apart overnight. {target_npc} has been passing messages through a crack in the stonework, but the guards patrol it constantly. If you can find the old tunnel {location_hint} and clear out the {enemy_type} that have nested in it, we could open a passage that no patrol would ever find.
dialogueChunks.outro: People on both sides are waiting for a chance to see each other again. All they need is a door.

playerResponses.text: A wall built on spite deserves to be opened. Show me this tunnel.
Tunnels full of monsters and political risk? I'll want proper payment.
Breaking barriers between settlements is above my pay grade.
